doc_name: What to Expect after an Inspection: 483s, Responses and Beyond

chunks.doc_name: What to Expect after an Inspection: 483s, Responses and Beyond
chunks.text: # What to Expect after an Inspection: 483s, Responses and Beyond ## U.S. Food and Drug Administration 194000 subscribers ### Description 8275 views Posted: 20 Dec 2022 During this webinar, FDA provided an overview of what to expect after a compounding inspection. FDA discussed the intent of an FDA Form 483, ‘Inspectional Observation,’ compounding inspection closeout meetings, post-inspection expectations and regulatory responses, and provided a few examples to help explain how best to respond to an FDA Form 483 observation after a compounding inspection. Timestamps 07:07 – Rebecca Asente, MS, RD - What to Expect After an Inspection 31:02 – Jennifer DelValleOrtiz, MS - Discussion of Examples 42:58 – Q&A Discussion Panel 59:16 – FDA Compounding Quality Center of Excellence Speakers: Rebecca Asente, MS, RD Compliance Officer Office of Pharmaceutical Quality Operations (OPQO) Office of Regulatory Affairs (ORA) | US FDA Jennifer DelValleOrtiz, MS Supervisory Consumer Safety Officer Division of Compounding I (DC1) Office of Compounding Quality & Compliance (OCQC) Office of Compliance (OC) Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER) | US FDA Jill Hammond Captain, US Public Health Service Program Manager Office of Compounding Quality & Compliance (OCQC) Office of Compliance (OC) Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)| US FDA Panelists: Rebecca Asente and Jennifer DelValleOrtiz Learn more at: https://www.fda.gov/drugs/news-events-human-drugs/what-expect-after-inspection-483s-responses-and-beyond-12142022 ----------------------- FDA CDER’s Small Business and Industry Assistance (SBIA) educates and provides assistance in understanding the regulatory aspects of human drug products & clinical research. Upcoming Training - https://www.fda.gov/cdersbia SBIA Listserv - https://public.govdelivery.com/accounts/USFDA/subscriber/new?topic_id=USFDA_352 SBIA 2022 Playlist - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLey4Qe-UxcxbzCGn90m38cXN2DL2i3VmD SBIA LinkedIn - https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/cder-small-business-and-industry-assistance SBIA Training Resources - https://www.fda.gov/cdersbialearn Twitter - https://twitter.com/FDA_Drug_Info Email - CDERSBIA@fda.hhs.gov Phone - (301) 796-6707 I (866) 405-5367 ### Transcript: good day everyone and welcome thank you for joining us today for our presentation on what to inspect what to expect after an inspection 483's responses and Beyond during this webinar FDA will provide an overview of what to expect after a compounding inspection my name is Ray Ford and I'm with the small business and industry assistance program which operates within the division of drug information which in turn resides in fda's Center for drug evaluation research office of communications I'll serve as your host and moderator for today's webinar for your convenience this slide provides the QR codes for our social media Avenues you can scan them now with your cell phones and be taken to the appropriate platform before I begin I'd like to cover some details slides for today's presentation are available from the right hand side of your screen or the orange button says download the presentation this activity is eligible for an attendance certificate which supports continuing education by Silka wraps sqa and acrp please refer to our website at fde.gov forward slash Cedar sbia for more details you'll only be able to obtain the attendance certificate upon completion of the survey which women open for two weeks please remember to download the certificate immediately upon completing the survey it cannot be retrieved after the fact this also means the survey will close on December the 28th even if you don't

issuance of written objectionable observations is mandate mandated by law and agency policy and you can find this in the food drug and cosmetics act section 704 B observations cited on the 483 are not an exhaustive list of objectionable conditions that may be present ultimately firms are responsible for conducting internal self-audits to continually identify and correct all violations of applicable laws and regulations and to improve the overall quality of operations the observations documented on the FDA 483 may be symptomatic of serious problems and it is up to the firm to investigate and determine the root causes of the violations and take prompt actions to correct them to bring operations and Drug products into compliance FDA investigators are charged with bringing any objectionable findings to your attention and these findings need to be addressed and corrected by you including any additional concerns of significance which may be discussed with you but not documented on the 483 supervisor Often by this time the investigator is preparing and traveling to were involved in another inspection and we usually end up receiving your 483 response somewhere in the time period between the writing of the report and when the report is being reviewed and once endorsed the report will be reviewed by the assigned compliance officer and during their review the compliance officer may also have questions for the investigator after the review by the office of Regulatory Affairs the work is reviewed by the center for drug evaluation and research Cedar there will be several offices within Cedar that will need to review the material during this period it is common for us to respond generally to your firms inquiries for example if you refer emails the or Ora compliance Branch requesting a meeting we would likely not Grant meetings with firms as the inspection evidence and the regulatory response continue to develop during this time we would not be able to provide you with any conclusive replies so this is likely not a period when FDA would be meeting with you a meeting if held would likely not be scheduled until after this process is complete and to this whole process other inspectional factors including immediate actions such as our needing to reach out and maybe recommend a recall activity all of this would affect time frames and in rare instances we do collect
